description: Shared bridge/link-speed network contract fixture between fivenines-agent and fivenines-server (issue #50: bridge saturation monitoring). Source of truth: fivenines-agent tests/fixtures/network_contract_payload.json, asserted end-to-end by tests/test_network.py::test_contract_fixture_round_trip -- with os_family forced to 'linux', interfaces()/psutil.net_io_counters mocked from 'interfaces'/'counters', and /sys/class/net modelled by 'sysfs', network() must equal payload['network']. The server ingests data['network'] == payload['network']; it consumes the new per-interface keys (interface_type, network_link_speed_bps, bridge_member_count, bridge). Saturation = rate(bytes)/network_link_speed_bps is computed for every interface whose network_link_speed_bps is non-null; a null value is 'unknown' and skips the calc. Saturation must key off network_link_speed_bps presence, NOT interface_type == 'physical' -- interface_type is a coarse grouping hint (a bond or vlan carrying the host uplink is labelled 'virtual'; paravirtual NICs are 'physical'), so gating the calc on it would drop real uplinks. 'interfaces', 'counters' and 'sysfs' are agent-side inputs the server ignores. Change the payload shape only in lockstep across both repos.
